text: One of the most hilariously ridiculous moments of 2025 had to be Schroinger's Kennedy, who seems to have been living in a quantum state of fired, retired, fully employed, and battling for control of Lucasfilm for the past 5 years. Clickbait news channels and content creators have been confidently predicting her imminent departure since the days of The Last Jedi. And yet, it seems that rumors of her career death were greatly exaggerated. Year after year, failure after failure, there she

remained, the proverbial turd that just wouldn't flush. But then in early 2025, serious rumors began to circulate amongst mainstream news outlets that the long- aaited event was finally happening. Kathleen Kennedy was stepping down as president of Lucas Film.

It all got so ridiculous that she actually had to issue a statement angrily denying the rumors, reassuring everyone that she'd never stop producing movies and that she had no immediate plans to step down as president. [screaming]

Because if there's one thing you need to understand about Kathleen Kennedy, it's that she'll leave when she's godamn good and ready, not a second before. And she'll leave under terms of her own choosing. She was never going to get fired. She was never going to get demoted or sidelined. And she was never going to be politely hurried out the door. She was too powerful. She was too well-connected. And she was too female to ever face actual consequences of her actions. Honestly, I wouldn't be

surprised if the rumors were actually true. But because they stole her thunder and all the celebrations exposed just how unpopular she was, she decided to stay on just a bit longer out of pure [ __ ] spite. >> Yes. Yes. >> But hey, I guess you can only fight the clock for so long. And the new year brought a new batch of rumors from Matthew Bologna. Matthew at Puck news that after 14 not particularly glorious years at the helm, Kathleen Kennedy is finally stepping

down again. A [ __ ] here we go again. Or is she? I mean, Jesus. After more false starts than a [ __ ] Ford escort, I'm very much in the camp now of I'll believe it when I see it. At this point, it feels like she'd rather stay on until she dies of sheer old age and then hang around for a few weeks beyond that weekend at Bernie style before she'd ever give anyone the satisfaction of reporting on her retirement. But what the hell? Let's humor the rumor and see

what young Matthew has for us here. So, Kennedy's due to announce that she's stepping down as president of Lucasfilm at some point this year, apparently. When? Don't know. Will she retire completely or move into a producer role so she can more directly [ __ ] up the films under her control? Unclear. What is clear from the report is that someone has to step in and replace her. And who might that be? Well, the most obvious and also perhaps the least inspiring choice imaginable, Dave Fona.

Well, technically Dave Fona and Lynwin Brennan as kind of co-presidents. Because let's be honest here, the idea of that [ __ ] [ __ ] running an entire studio by himself is like putting Kim Kardashian in charge of the United Nations. Now, as far as rumors go, it's definitely one of the more believable and less outlandish options available out there. We all knew Filelon was being groomed. Still feels weird saying that one in a Hollywood context. As her successor for years now, from Kennedy's

perspective, he probably seems like a safe bet to carry on her legacy. He's not some wild aur filmmaker who's going to make crazy creative decisions, that's for sure. And he doesn't strike me as some hard-headed tough-talking go-getter who's going to reshape the entire studio and do things his way, godamn it. He's a solid, predictable company man who's been part of the Lucasfilm system for like 20 years now. He knows how to play the game, and he's probably become very

good at kissing Kennedy's ass and telling her exactly what she wants to hear. He was handpicked by George Lucas himself back in the day. So, there's a kind of tenuous continuity with Georgie's original vision there. Another big plus that helps to sell him to the fan base. Ahsoka with its endless girl bosses and stoic female leads is definitely proof of his feminist credentials. So, that's another massive plus for Kathy. >> Put a ticket in. Make a living gay.

>> And after working together for so long, they probably have the kind of personal relationship where he wouldn't object to a bit of creative input from her once in a while. got to make sure he doesn't start getting ideas of his own and forgets to push the message. Now, I don't know if I can apply the word creative to a man like Dave Fona, but it's probably the most accurate description of what he's theoretically good at. But one thing he definitely doesn't understand is business. Hence,

the inclusion of Lynwin Brennan to handle all the thinkythinky stuff. Again, this kind of makes sense in theory at least. Brennan is the current head of Lucasfilm business. And as far as I can tell, she comes mostly from a technology and visual effects background, working her way up to become head of ILM before transferring over to Lucasfilm. She'll be overseeing the corporate and executive side of things, allowing Dave to do what he does best, like uh again, as far as rumors go, it does

have a grain of logic and common sense to it. Admittedly, a tiny one, but a grain nonetheless. Whether it'll actually happen or whether Kennedy will once again decide she's not quite ready to leave the spotlight is anyone's guess at this point. I mean, she did say previously that she wanted to go out on a high with a big win to pad her ego and reassure herself that she's not the hapless diversity hire [ __ ] that everyone believes her to be. We all assumed that win might come in the form

of season 2 of Andor, which didn't exactly rock the world of film making to its core, but it did at least get a great critical response. But I guess not. Then again, we do have the Mandalorian movie coming out in a few months, and as far as Star Wars projects go, that's pretty much the biggest thing they've got on the horizon. But if she's holding out for an epic critical and financial win from that thing, well, she might be around for a long time to come in that case.
Anyway, that's all I've got for today. Go away now.
